text: In this video, we present robotic complete mesocolic excision with high vascular tie of the iliocolic, right colic, and middle colic artery with intracorporeal side-to-side anastomosis with omental pedicle flap.

A seventy-four-year-old male patient diagnosed with ascending colon cancer previously had bilateral inguinal hernia repair, no distant metastasis identified preoperatively. He has significant comorbidities including obstructive sleep apnea requiring continuous positive airway pressure, hypopituitarism, pseudogood of right knee.

The patient is positioned in a modified lithotomy position following endotracheal intubation. Four 8mm robotic ports, one 8mm air seal port, and one 5mm assistant port are visibly placed for the procedure.

The surgeon uses robotic scissors, grasper, and bipolar instruments to facilitate dissection of the ileocolic vessels. The dissection progresses from caudal to cranial and medial to lateral, tracing the ileocolic pedicle to the level of the superior mesenteric artery (SMA). The ileocolic vein is identified and ligated, followed by separate ligation of the artery.
The surgeon identifies the midcolic pedicle and ligates the right-sided branches. The loop of Henle is then dissected and clipped using surgical instruments.

The surgeon performs dissection from right to left, separating the omentum from the colon. The transection site is identified using indocyanine green with the Firefly imaging system. Robotic scissors are replaced by a robotic stapler, and the colon is transected using the stapler.

The surgeon places the completely separated colon specimen on top of the liver. An isoperistaltic site-to-site anastomosis is created using an additional staple, and two bowel segments are fixed together with 3-0 Vicryl sutures.

An anisoperistaltic side-to-side anastomosis is created using a staple, with common enterotomies made on the colon and small bowel using scissors. The left upper quadrant trocar is downsized from twelve millimeters to eight millimeters. The enterotomy site is closed with a running suture using 3-0 Vicryl, supplemented by additional interrupted sutures.

The surgeon prepares the omental pedicle graft and wraps it around the anastomosis site, ensuring final hemostasis. A twelve-millimeter suprapubic trocar is inserted, and the specimen is extracted from the abdominal cavity.

There was no postoperative complication and the patient was discharged from the hospital at postoperative day two. Histopathological evaluation revealed T-one tumor without lymph node metastasis with clear surgical margins.
